purpose: v1 expanded held-out set: 12 positives (3 per relation class) + 6 negatives (3 topic-mention, 2 restatement, 1 coexistence). Answer key frozen before any model saw any case.
item_id_rule: IDs are assigned by memory_extractor in bullet order as M001, M002, etc.

cases.text:
# Backup Policy

- Nightly backups are kept for 30 days on the legacy NAS under the operations share.
- Effective this quarter the old retention rule is replaced: nightly backups are kept for 90 days in the cloud archive, and the legacy NAS copies are discontinued.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: the old retention rule is replaced: nightly backups are kept for 90 days in the cloud archive

cases.text:
# Secrets Handling

- Service API keys are rotated manually every quarter by the platform team.
- New standard: the manual quarterly rotation is retired. Service API keys now rotate automatically every 30 days through the secrets manager.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: the manual quarterly rotation is retired. Service API keys now rotate automatically every 30 days

cases.text:
# Team Communication

- Weekly sync notes are posted to the shared drive folder for the whole team to read.
- Update: posting sync notes to the shared drive is deprecated. Weekly sync notes must be posted to the project wiki instead.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: posting sync notes to the shared drive is deprecated. Weekly sync notes must be posted to the project wiki instead.

cases.text:
# Support Refunds

- Support agents may issue refunds up to 200 dollars at their own discretion.
- Revised policy: support agents may issue refunds only up to 50 dollars on their own, and anything higher requires a team lead's approval.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: support agents may issue refunds only up to 50 dollars on their own

cases.text:
# Database Access

- Backend engineers may query the production database for debugging purposes.
- Tightened rule: backend engineers may query only the read replica of the production database, and only during an active incident.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: backend engineers may query only the read replica of the production database, and only during an active incident.

cases.text:
# Marketing Publishing

- The marketing intern can publish posts to the company social accounts.
- Going forward the intern may publish only pre-approved drafts to the company social accounts, never original posts.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: the intern may publish only pre-approved drafts to the company social accounts

cases.text:
# Logging Rules

- Request logs should include the full customer email address to speed up support lookups.
- Customer email addresses must never be written to request logs under any circumstances.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: Customer email addresses must never be written to request logs under any circumstances.

cases.text:
# Release Practices

- Teams are encouraged to deploy to production on Friday afternoons to use the quiet window.
- Production deploys are forbidden on Fridays; schedule them Monday through Thursday only.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: Production deploys are forbidden on Fridays

cases.text:
# Vendor Relations

- Engineers should contact the payments vendor directly whenever an integration question comes up.
- Engineers must not contact the payments vendor directly; every request goes through the partnerships desk.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: Engineers must not contact the payments vendor directly

cases.text:
# Incident Process

- The on-call engineer declares incident severity when an outage begins.
- After the process review, only the incident commander declares incident severity. The on-call engineer reports symptoms but no longer makes the call.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: only the incident commander declares incident severity. The on-call engineer reports symptoms but no longer makes the call.

cases.text:
# Purchasing

- Team leads approve software purchases under 1000 dollars from the tools budget.
- Purchasing authority has moved: the finance office now approves all software purchases from the tools budget. Team leads submit requests but do not approve them.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: the finance office now approves all software purchases from the tools budget. Team leads submit requests but do not approve them.

cases.text:
# Content Publishing

- The blog editor gives final signoff on every article before it is published.
- Final signoff on every article now belongs to the legal reviewer. The blog editor prepares drafts but no longer gives the final signoff.

expected_relations.cited_evidence_span: Final signoff on every article now belongs to the legal reviewer.

cases.text:
# Onboarding Materials

- The onboarding workshop teaches new agents how to spot when one instruction supersedes another in a memory file.
- The workshop materials include worked examples of superseded rules, narrowed scopes, and transferred authority for practice.

expected_no_relations.reason: training material discusses authority changes as a subject; nothing here changes any rule

cases.text:
# Team Changelog

- The team changelog notes that last month several outdated instructions were retired from the assistant's memory.
- The changelog entry describes that cleanup as an example of catching contradictions early; it does not itself retire any instruction in this file.

expected_no_relations.reason: historical narrative about past retirements; no rule in this file changes another

cases.text:
# Research Notes

- Our research paper argues that agent memory needs an authority layer to catch superseded and contradicted instructions.
- Reviewer two asked us to clarify that the paper analyzes authority transfer as a failure class rather than transferring any authority itself.

expected_no_relations.reason: research meta-discussion of authority change; nothing enacted

cases.text:
# Privacy Rules

- All customer data exports require the privacy lead's written approval before they run.
- Current rule, restated for the new quarter: customer data exports still require the privacy lead's written approval before they run. Nothing about this policy has changed.

expected_no_relations.reason: reaffirmation with update-flavored language; the rule is unchanged, so there is no authority-change relation

cases.text:
# Security Baseline

- Two-factor authentication is mandatory for every admin account on the platform.
- Reminder from the security review: two-factor authentication remains mandatory for every admin account on the platform, exactly as before.

expected_no_relations.reason: reminder restating an unchanged rule; reaffirmation is not an authority change

cases.text:
# Operations Notes

- Expense reports are submitted through the finance portal by the last day of the month.
- Conference talk proposals need a manager's thumbs-up before submission to the events team.

expected_no_relations.reason: two rules in unrelated scopes merely coexist; no shared scope, no authority-change relation
